item.title: ops/security/ directory and sanitizer audit logs created without secure modes — quarantined input and injection attempts world-readable
item.intent: The ops/security/ directory must be created with mode 0700 and sanitizer-audit.jsonl/sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl must be written with mode 0600 — these files record quarantined content and injection attempts that must not be readable by other users.
item.description: src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts creates DEFAULT_OPS_DIR (~/.clawhq/ops/security/) on-demand via ensureDir in sanitizer/audit.ts. ensureDir calls mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option (line 51 of sanitizer/audit.ts), creating ops/security/ at default 0755 permissions — world-listable. The sanitizer-audit.jsonl and sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl files are written by appendFile without mode, creating them at 0644 — world-readable. ops/security/ is NOT in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET (it's a different path from the security/ root directory which is in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET). These files record security scan results including flagged PII, injection attempts, and quarantined inputs — sensitive security intelligence that other users must not read. Fix: change mkdir in sanitizer/audit.ts to pass mode DIR_MODE_SECRET, add chmodSync for existing installs, change appendFile to use mode FILE_MODE_SECRET.
steps: Read src/secure/sanitizer/audit.ts lines 1-60 — confirm mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) at ~line 51 has no mode option, appendFile at ~line 57 has no mode option, and neither chmodSync nor DIR_MODE_SECRET nor FILE_MODE_SECRET are imported
Read src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts lines 23-40 — confirm DEFAULT_OPS_DIR is ~/.clawhq/ops/security/ (NOT ~/.clawhq/security/) and that this path is not in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET
Add chmodSync to the node:fs/promises import (or add a separate sync import from node:fs) in sanitizer/audit.ts — note: appendFile is async (fs/promises), but chmodSync is sync (node:fs)
Add import { chmodSync } from 'node:fs' to sanitizer/audit.ts (separate from the fs/promises import)
Add import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js to sanitizer/audit.ts
Change mkdir(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdir(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }) in the ensureDir function in sanitizer/audit.ts
Add chmodSync(dir, DIR_MODE_SECRET) after the await mkdir call in ensureDir — fixes existing installs where ops/security/ was created at 0755
Change appendFile(filePath, ..., 'utf-8') to appendFile(filePath, ..., { encoding: 'utf-8', mode: FILE_MODE_SECRET }) in the appendJsonl function — this sets the mode on file creation
Run the full test suite — confirm all sanitizer tests pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: ops/security/ directory is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read or execute bits
sanitizer-audit.jsonl and sanitizer-quarantine.jsonl are created with mode 0600 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
